channel.title: Statistics on Jens Rantil
channel.description: Recent content in Statistics on Jens Rantil

item.title: MTTR is the wrong metric
item.description: Shower thought: Why do we talk about MTTR (Mean Time To Resolution), when it’s a latency? Wouldn’t it make more sense to talk about P99TTR (99th percentile Time To Resolution) given that latencies usually have a long tail? 🤔 #MTTR #SRE #DevOps — Jens Rantil (@JensRantil) March 10, 2021 Today I would like to talk about why Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) is a wrong metric to look at. For the past few years many software engineers have been using the DORA metrics to track the performance of software delivery.

item.title: Mean vs. median
item.description: Over the years, I have heard many people say something like Over 50% of people think they are better than the average car driver. They say this as if it’s funny or sounds smart. But the fact is that actually could be true, and the reason is because mean and median are not the same thing. When people take a basic course in statistics, they are likely to come out of it thinking that the world mostly consists of normal distributions.
